title: New ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure
description: Security researchers have identified a new ransomware variant specifically designed to target cloud-based infrastructure. The campaign uses compromised CI/CD pipelines to deploy malicious code.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity.

title: Show HN: Open-source vulnerability scanner
description: New tool automatically scans dependencies for known vulnerabilities and suggests patches. Gaining traction in the security community.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The open-source vulnerability scanner itself does not pose a direct threat but could be exploited if malicious actors modify or distribute compromised versions. Additionally, over-reliance on such tools without proper validation could lead to missed vulnerabilities or false positives.
recommended_actions: Evaluate the tool's source code and verify its integrity before use.
Incorporate the tool into a broader security strategy, ensuring manual validation of its findings.
Monitor for any updates or advisories regarding potential vulnerabilities in the tool itself.

title: A flaw has been found in SourceCodester Comment System 1.0. This issue affects some unknown...

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-8126.

title: A security vulnerability has been detected in GPAC up to 26.02.0. This affects the function...
description: A security vulnerability has been detected in GPAC up to 26.02.0. This affects the function sidx_box_read of the file src/isomedia/box_code_base.c. The manipulation leads to allocation of resources. The attack must be carried out locally. The exploit has been disclosed publicly and may be used. The identifier of the patch is 442e2299530138d8f874fd885c565ba98a6318ba. It is suggested to install a patch to address this issue.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in GPAC up to version 26.02.0 could allow local attackers to exploit the sidx_box_read function, leading to resource allocation issues. While the exploit requires local access and has a low severity rating, it could still disrupt operations or be leveraged in targeted attacks.
recommended_actions: Apply the patch identified by commit 442e2299530138d8f874fd885c565ba98a6318ba to mitigate the vulnerability.
Restrict local access to systems running vulnerable versions of GPAC to trusted personnel only.
Monitor for any unusual resource allocation behavior on affected systems and review system logs for signs of exploitation.

title: An Exposed IOCTL with Insufficient Access Control vulnerability in AsusPTPFilter allows a local...
description:
An Exposed IOCTL with Insufficient Access Control vulnerability in AsusPTPFilter allows a local user to bypass driver security mechanisms and obtain restricted touchpad information or render the touchpad unusable via crafted IOCTL requests.Refer to the '
Security Update for ASUS Precision Touchpad ' section on the ASUS Security Advisory for more information.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows a local user to bypass security mechanisms in the AsusPTPFilter driver, potentially accessing restricted touchpad information or disabling the touchpad. This could lead to minor disruptions or privacy concerns but is limited to local exploitation.
recommended_actions: Apply the security update provided in the ASUS Security Advisory for the Precision Touchpad immediately.
Restrict local user access to systems until the patch is applied to mitigate exploitation risks.
Monitor system logs for unusual IOCTL requests targeting the AsusPTPFilter driver.

title: A vulnerability was found in SourceCodester SUP Online Shopping 1.0. The affected element is an...
description: A vulnerability was found in SourceCodester SUP Online Shopping 1.0. The affected element is an unknown function of the file /admin/viewmsg.php. Performing a manipulation of the argument msgid results in sql injection. The attack is possible to be carried out remotely. The exploit has been made public and could be used.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows remote attackers to exploit an SQL injection flaw in the /admin/viewmsg.php file by manipulating the 'msgid' parameter. This could lead to unauthorized access to sensitive data, database compromise, or potential full system compromise.
recommended_actions: Immediately apply input validation and parameterized queries to prevent SQL injection.
Restrict access to the /admin/viewmsg.php endpoint to authorized users only and monitor logs for unusual activity.
Check for and apply any available patches or updates from the SourceCodester SUP Online Shopping 1.0 vendor or community.

title: An Out-of-bounds Read vulnerability in the IOCTL handler in ASUS System Control Interface allows...
description:
An Out-of-bounds Read vulnerability in the IOCTL handler in ASUS System Control Interface allows a local user to cause system crash (BSOD) via a read size that exceeds the buffer size.Refer to the '
Security Update for MyASUS ' section on the ASUS Security Advisory for more information.

title: A vulnerability was found in Open5GS up to 2.7.7. This affects the function...
description: A vulnerability was found in Open5GS up to 2.7.7. This affects the function ogs_sbi_discovery_option_add_service_names in the library /lib/sbi/message.c of the component NSSF. The manipulation results in denial of service. The attack may be performed from remote. The exploit has been made public and could be used. The project was informed of the problem early through an issue report but has not responded yet.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in Open5GS up to version 2.7.7 could allow remote attackers to cause a denial of service (DoS) by exploiting a flaw in the ogs_sbi_discovery_option_add_service_names function. This could disrupt network slicing and service discovery in 5G networks, potentially impacting service availability.
recommended_actions: Immediately upgrade Open5GS to a version above 2.7.7 if available, or apply any available patches or mitigations.
Implement network monitoring to detect and mitigate potential DoS attacks targeting this vulnerability.
Restrict access to the affected NSSF component to trusted IP ranges to reduce the attack surface.

title: A security vulnerability has been detected in SourceCodester Pizzafy Ecommerce System 1.0. This...
description: A security vulnerability has been detected in SourceCodester Pizzafy Ecommerce System 1.0. This issue affects some unknown processing of the file /admin/index.php. Such manipulation of the argument page leads to cross site scripting. The attack may be launched remotely. The exploit has been disclosed publicly and may be used.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in SourceCodester Pizzafy Ecommerce System 1.0 allows for cross-site scripting (XSS) attacks via the 'page' parameter in /admin/index.php. This could enable attackers to inject malicious scripts, potentially leading to data theft, session hijacking, or defacement of the admin interface.
recommended_actions: Apply input validation and sanitization on the 'page' parameter to prevent malicious scripts from being executed.
Update the application to the latest version if a patch is available or contact the vendor for a security fix.
Implement a web application firewall (WAF) to detect and block malicious requests targeting this vulnerability.

title: A weakness has been identified in huangjunsen0406 xiaozhi-mcphub up to 1.0.3. This vulnerability...
description: A weakness has been identified in huangjunsen0406 xiaozhi-mcphub up to 1.0.3. This vulnerability affects unknown code of the file src/controllers/dxtController.ts. This manipulation of the argument manifest.name causes path traversal. The attack may be initiated remotely. The exploit has been made available to the public and could be used for attacks. The project was informed of the problem early through an issue report but has not responded yet.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in huangjunsen0406 xiaozhi-mcphub up to version 1.0.3 allows for path traversal attacks via the manipulation of the 'manifest.name' argument in the src/controllers/dxtController.ts file. This could enable attackers to access unauthorized files or directories on the server, potentially exposing sensitive information. The exploit is publicly available, increasing the likelihood of its use in attacks.
recommended_actions: Immediately upgrade or patch the affected software to mitigate the vulnerability, if a fix becomes available.
Implement input validation and sanitization to prevent path traversal attacks in the application.
Monitor systems for suspicious activity and unauthorized access attempts, especially related to file paths.

title:
This vulnerability, in the MAXHUB Pivot client application versions 
prior to v1.36.2, may allow...
description:
This vulnerability, in the MAXHUB Pivot client application versions 
prior to v1.36.2, may allow an attacker to obtain encrypted tenant email
 addresses and related metadata from any tenant. Due to the presence of a
 hardcoded AES key within the application, the encrypted data can be 
decrypted, enabling access to tenant email addresses and associated 
information in cleartext. Furthermore, an attacker may be able to cause a
 denial-of-service condition by enrolling multiple unauthorized devices

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-6411.

title: A server-side request forgery (SSRF) vulnerability was identified in the GitHub Enterprise Server...
description: A server-side request forgery (SSRF) vulnerability was identified in the GitHub Enterprise Server notebook viewer that allowed an attacker to access internal services by exploiting URL parser confusion between the validation layer and the HTTP request library. The hostname validation used a different URL parser than the request library, enabling a crafted URL to pass validation while directing the request to an unintended host. Exploitation required network access to the GitHub Enterprise Server

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The SSRF vulnerability in GitHub Enterprise Server's notebook viewer could allow attackers to access internal services, potentially leading to data exfiltration, unauthorized access to sensitive systems, or further exploitation of internal infrastructure. This poses a significant risk to organizations relying on GitHub Enterprise Server for code hosting and collaboration.
recommended_actions: Apply the latest security patch provided by GitHub to address CVE-2026-8034 immediately.
Restrict network access to GitHub Enterprise Server to trusted IP ranges and implement strict firewall rules.
Conduct a security review of internal services accessible from the GitHub Enterprise Server to identify potential risks from SSRF exploitation.

title: A vulnerability was found in 8421bit MiniClaw up to 223c16a1088e138838dcbd18cd65a37c35ac5a84....
description: A vulnerability was found in 8421bit MiniClaw up to 223c16a1088e138838dcbd18cd65a37c35ac5a84. Affected is the function executeCognitivePulse of the file src/kernel.ts. Performing a manipulation results in os command injection. It is possible to initiate the attack remotely. The exploit has been made public and could be used. This product is using a rolling release to provide continious delivery. Therefore, no version details for affected nor updated releases are available. The patch is named 028

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows for OS command injection via the executeCognitivePulse function in the src/kernel.ts file. This could enable attackers to execute arbitrary commands on the affected system, potentially leading to unauthorized access, data exfiltration, or service disruption. The exploit is public, increasing the risk of exploitation.
recommended_actions: Immediately apply the patch named '028' to mitigate the vulnerability.
Conduct a security audit of systems running 8421bit MiniClaw to identify potential exploitation.
Implement network-level monitoring and intrusion detection to identify any suspicious activity targeting this vulnerability.

title: CVE-2026-22726 - Route Services can be leveraged to send app traffic to network destinations outside of an app's conf
description:
Route Services can be leveraged to send app traffic to network destinations outside of an app's configured egress rules. As a result, a malicious developer with access to Cloudfoundry could configure a route-service that would allow it to send requests to HTTP services on internal networks reachable by the Gorouter, which may not have previously had direct access from outside networks, or from the application.
Routing release: affected from v0.118.0 through v0.371.0 (inclusive); upgrade to v0.372.0 or greater. CF Deployment: affected from v0.0.2 through v54.14.0 (inclusive); upgrade to v55.0.0 or greater (includes routing_release v0.372.0).

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-22726.
